channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kamloops residential school claims spark debate over free speech and censorship in Canada
item.description: This opinion piece from the National Post examines the controversy surrounding public discourse on residential schools following the 2021 announcement by Tk’emlúps te Secwépemc First Nation regarding ...

item.title: Gladys Wanga Urges Respect for Women Leaders in Open Letter to James Orengo
item.description: Homa Bay Governor Gladys Wanga has publicly addressed Siaya Governor James Orengo through an emotional open letter, calling for dignity, reflection, and greater respect towards women serving in leader...

item.title: Naegohyang Women’s FC wins AFC Asian Women’s Champions League title in South Korea
item.description: Naegohyang Women’s FC of North Korea captured the AFC Asian Women’s Champions League title after competing in South Korea, marking a significant achievement for the club and for North Korean women’s f...

item.title: Cockroach Janta Party founder alleges website removal, social media hacks and account suspensions
item.description: Abhijit Dipke, founder of the satirical youth-led campaign Cockroach Janta Party, claimed on Saturday that the organisation’s website had been taken down and several of its social media accounts had e...

item.title: Displaced immigrants in Durban refuse to return home after xenophobic attacks
item.description: Hundreds of immigrants and refugees in Durban have spent several days displaced after being driven from their communities by anti-immigrant groups. Many of the affected people, including refugees from...

item.title: Kenyan Athletes Set for Key Diamond League Showdown in Xiamen After Shanghai Opener
item.description: Kenya will once again be strongly represented in the second leg of the 2026 Diamond League meeting scheduled for Xiamen, China, taking place shortly after the season opener in Shanghai. The event is a...

item.title: Gas explosion at Shanxi coal mine in China kills at least 90 workers
item.description: At least 90 people have been confirmed dead following a serious gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China’s Shanxi Province, an incident now described as the country’s deadliest mining accident i...

item.title: Tanzania PPAA to integrate e-Procurement and Judiciary systems to improve procurement dispute handling
item.description: Tanzania’s Public Procurement Appeals Authority (PPAA) is moving forward with major digital reforms aimed at improving how procurement complaints and appeals are handled in the country. The authority ...

item.title: Canada weighs deeper North American trade integration ahead of USMCA review talks
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is adjusting Canada’s trade strategy ahead of the upcoming review of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA). Although Carney previously argued that Canada’s heavy...

item.title: Obi Cubana refutes claims of leaving Tinubu’s City Boy Movement, reaffirms support
item.description: Nigerian businessman and socialite, Obinna Iyiegbu, popularly known as Obi Cubana, has dismissed viral rumours suggesting that he has distanced himself from President Bola Tinubu’s City Boy Movement. ...

item.title: Three Red Cross volunteers die from Ebola during humanitarian work in DRC
item.description: The Red Cross has paid tribute to three of its volunteers who died after contracting Ebola while carrying out humanitarian duties in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC). The volunteers were part of...

item.title: A reflection on Indian Railways through Amitava Kumar’s ‘The Social Life of Indian Trains’
item.description: The article is a reflective review of Amitava Kumar’s book ‘The Social Life of Indian Trains’, which examines Indian Railways as a space of intense human interaction, inequality, migration, memory, an...

item.title: Piers Morgan Urges Trump to Avoid Renewed Military Action Against Iran Amid Rising Speculation
item.description: British media personality Piers Morgan publicly urged President Donald Trump to avoid escalating tensions with Iran after reports and speculation suggested the United States and Israel could be prepar...

item.title: Nations begin announcing 26-man squads for expanded FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Countries taking part in the FIFA World Cup 2026 have started unveiling their 26-man squads ahead of the tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. This edition of the competition will featur...

item.title: Ontario NDP proposes removing provincial HST on all food and beverages, creating political pressure for Doug Ford
item.description: In this opinion column, Scott Stinson examines the Ontario NDP’s proposal to eliminate the provincial portion of the Harmonized Sales Tax (HST) on all food and beverages. NDP leader Marit Stiles annou...

item.title: Sowore leads Abuja protest against alleged land grabbing and calls for protection of Jabi Lake and public spaces
item.description: On Saturday, former presidential candidate and activist Omoyele Sowore led a large group of protesters in Abuja to demonstrate against what they described as widespread land grabbing within the Federa...

item.title: Gordon Ramsay Launches “Idiot Sandwich” Pop-Up Experience in BGC, Manila
item.description: Manila’s food scene has once again drawn global attention with the arrival of a unique pop-up experience inspired by celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay’s viral “Idiot Sandwich” meme. Located in Bonifacio Gl...

item.title: Opinion piece critiques media coverage of reports on sexual violence related to the Israel–Hamas conflict
item.description: This opinion article by Raheel Raza discusses the release of a report titled “Silence No More” by Israel’s Civil Commission on Oct. 7th Crimes by Hamas Against Women and Children, which documents alle...

item.title: IMD warns of continued heatwave over central India until end of May
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a warning that severe heatwave conditions are likely to continue over central India till the end of May 2026. According to the latest advisory, reg...

item.title: 1944 Volkswagen Type 82 Kübelwagen: Design and Role of Germany’s WWII Utility Vehicle
item.description: The 1944 Volkswagen Type 82 Kübelwagen was a key light utility vehicle used by German forces during World War II. Designed by Ferdinand Porsche, it was developed from the early Volkswagen Beetle platf...
